message:
⚡ acmm: cache badge + scan for 1 h to scale to 200+ badged repos (#8362)

The ACMM badge is starting to land in third-party READMEs (fullsend-ai,
AEF, Reflect outreach). Each badge URL gets re-evaluated by shields.io,
which then hits our Netlify badge function, which hits the scan
function, which hits the GitHub tree API. The prior cache windows
(badge 5 min · scan 15 min) meant 4 GitHub calls per badged repo per
hour — fine for ~50 repos, tight at 1000+.

Three aligned bumps so the chain caches uniformly at 1 h:

1. acmm-badge: Cache-Control max-age 300 → 3600
2. acmm-badge: add `cacheSeconds: 3600` to all three response shapes —
   shields.io respects this and stops re-fetching faster than our CDN.
3. acmm-scan: blob TTL 15 min → 60 min — keeps the badge function
   serving from cache for the full window.

Effect: max 1 GitHub call per badged repo per hour. 200 repos = 200/hr
= 4% of the 5000/hr authenticated token limit (verified GITHUB_TOKEN
is set in Netlify production context). Headroom for ~5000 active
badges before we'd need to switch to GitHub App auth.

ACMM level changes on the order of days (file-tree shape, not commit
activity), so the longer cache is invisible to users.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
